## [00:00 - 03:01]

So broad audiences are really right now a big opportunity, probably bigger than they've ever been. Why is that? Then on the other side, you have things like iOS 14.5, which cause issues with look-alike quality. So if you understand look-alikes, look-alikes need a seed data. So it needs seed data. So if you have a look-alike of a custom audience, that will generally be an email list. It could be a mobile phone list. It could be video viewers, things like that. So that look-alike audience, the quality has dropped on the one side. And then website customer audiences have massively dropped. So website customers, audiences are people that have visited a pixel event and therefore you've made a look-alike from that. So people that have purchased and hit the purchase pixel, people that have become a lead, people that have added to cart. So this is where now I believe broad has become even more important, but it's not as simple as saying, well, I'm just going to run a broad campaign and I'm going to create some great ads and things are going to be great. ## [30:22 - 33:26]

So when you are looking at broad audiences, and let's say you've run them before and they've worked for you or maybe they've stopped working, a lot of it will come down to iOS 14, some of the changes that have been made, the way that Facebook's able to collect data on people. And some of that has to be down to also whether you've given Facebook enough information on who you're trying to target as well. Now, one of the other strategies which also works, here's the thing, if you've got a Facebook rep, they'll often talk to you about this particular strategy. It's come up a lot. They've been, I remember they mentioned it to me many, many years ago. When you launch a new campaign or new ad account or new business, one of the things that Facebook tell you to do is to run traffic campaigns to your lander. And the idea behind this is get the traffic in, let the pixel learn who your target is, and then once the pixels learned, are learned, then you can actually run conversion or lead campaigns back to that lander and that pixel's taken the learnings, it's passed it back into Facebook and things like that, etc, etc. And so many people blast Facebook reps because they say this is terrible advice, you know, why would I want to waste money, waste traffic, waste ad spend and stuff like that. I just want purchases, just get me the purchases and things like that. There is some truth in this. And the truth here is if you go back to this and you're looking to enrich the data that Facebook has by getting people to visit your website, they may not buy, you're actually giving Facebook a hint as to the type of people that are the right people. So for example, in this case, one of the recommendations would be to run traffic to particularly interests or if you've got a custom audience, then create a lookalike and then start telling the pixel who you're actually after. In our case, what we actually do, and this is something especially for e-com and for lead gen, it's done slightly differently. I run traffic for add to cart because I want people coming to my site, they're actually going to stay and show consideration at that stage. I don't want people necessarily to buy and it's also part of the power behind graduation testing. So if you've run graduation testing, we often don't test on the purchase pixel, it's the pixel that's cheapest and can give us a lot of testing data as well. And therefore, it's kind of improving its model of your persona. And the better you do that,

the better your broad audiences work. Now, you might argue and say, well, I have no data to start with. Now, the other part of this is also what Facebook are looking at is when your ad is in the auction, you'll have heard of this algorithm, hopefully, if you've followed the BPM method, if you haven't, or you haven't quite got into the depths of how the algorithm works, I'll just give you a quick short primer on how this relates to broad as well. So B times EAR plus V is bid times estimated action rate plus user value. Now, the bid automatic or manual by default is automatic. You can override that and you can do cost cap and bid cap and all those kind of different bidding opportunities. The reason you need to bid is because Facebook doesn't think that your ad or your ad account or your campaign deserves the best users. So when you're running on auto and things are going well, you're kind of doing something really well. When it's not, then you're kind of overriding the auction and that's fine. And sometimes you need to do that. Estimated action rate, this is why it's really important to prime that pixel because what Facebook says here is what's the chance that the event you're optimizing for is going to occur. So if you launch straight into purchase for a broad campaign, the likelihood is kind of close to zero because it has no data on your site. It's also going to be low for ad to cart. But when you're optimizing for ad to cart, as you get more and more and more hits, the estimated action rate starts to increase and Facebook gets more confident in serving visitors to your website so that when you launch a new campaign for purchase, it already has some data. So your estimated action rate starts to increase. And then the other part here is what's the user value? And one of the tricks here, which some of you will already be doing

## [36:30 - 38:19]

anyway, is running page post engagement ads to these ads that you're priming to broad audiences based on your goal. Because the other part of this is what's the actual user response from your ads, looking at bounce rate from your landing page and things like that. And page post engagement, especially going to the same audience and just getting those likes, shares and comments, goes a long way to tell Facebook, you know what, the targeting is good here. And it will start to understand who your best user is. And when you've primed all of these and put all of these in place, maybe surprise, surprise, maybe no surprise, broad will start working far better for you. I don't know if it's going to be working as good as your look likes are or were or your interest. But if you get these things in play, your broad targeting will work 10 or 100 times better than it ever did. And I don't believe from all the things I've read, all the forums on part of all the masterminds and things like that, anyone truly understands the power abroad like this and is able to implement it and make it work. And I'd encourage you to make sure that you're following this, whether it's your website or your working clients, to make sure that step by step, you get these things in place, it will make a huge difference because when you can make broad targeting work, your job's so much easier. You can focus far more on testing copy and creative and landing page and offer and promotions and upsells and cross sells. That's where the money's to be made, not by tweaking audiences and doing multiple ad sets and bidding and things like that. That comes kind of afterwards and over time will have less and less impact. But this is one of the biggest opportunities I believe we have right now to make Facebook ads work on broad like clockwork.
